steps.title: Spot the two-part movement
steps.content: The passage has two distinct movements: first it introduces and describes the Hibulb Cultural Center and the Turtle Mountain Band of Chippewa center, then it pivots with “Both centers contrast with...” to set these tribal institutions against a different category—non-Indigenous museums.

steps.title: Match the structure to the choices
steps.content: C captures this movement exactly: “discusses two cultural centers operated by tribes” maps to the opening sentences about Hibulb and Turtle Mountain, and “then compares them with non-Indigenous institutions that present Indigenous exhibits” maps to the final contrast. A misframes the passage as analyzing how each group designed exhibits of particular artifacts—the passage isn't artifact-specific. B invents audience response, which the passage never discusses. D reverses the logic, treating the two tribal centers as representing old and new strategies, when in fact they both use the same approach and are contrasted together against a third category. That matches choice C — C is correct.
